key_insight: Graph neural networks are now a general-purpose tool for relational data, but their success depends on when graph structure truly adds value and on matching the architecture to the domain.
engineering_relevance: For materials discovery and related scientific workflows, GNNs can improve predictive modeling when structure is meaningful, but deployment requires careful graph construction, baseline comparison, and attention to scalability and robustness.
method: Organize the field by a unified GNN design space spanning spectral and spatial formulations.
Map expressive power to the Weisfeiler-Leman hierarchy to clarify what models can and cannot distinguish.
Compare architecture choices across 12 domains, with emphasis on graph construction cost, heterophily, and temporal complexity.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a cross-domain survey across twelve application areas and evaluates reported gains against baseline quality, split choice, and deployment realities.
watch_item: Strong leaderboard results may not transfer to real-world use because heterophily, scale, temporal graphs, oversmoothing, and distribution shift can undermine performance.
paper_title: Graph Neural Networks Applications Across Domains: All Insights You Need
